summary: Stancil uses a dataset of Wordle scores shared on Twitter to show that most players win easily and that "hard mode"—reusing known greens—actually performs better than more exploratory strategies. He then pivots to the bigger lesson: data analysis is shaped by the questions we choose and the paths we ignore, so truth-telling requires acknowledging those biases.
money_quotes: The overwhelming conclusion that they tell us is this: You shouldn’t lose.
Evidently, playing strategically is foolish, and playing on hard mode is easier.
The final product was a child of these choices.
Nobody goes digging looking for dirt.
key_insight: Data can illuminate optimal play in Wordle, but the deeper takeaway is that analysis is never fully objective because the questions we ask—and skip—shape the truths we tell.
